metadata.name: FERS Special Retirement Supplement (SRS) Rules
metadata.description: Rules, eligibility, formula, earnings test, and tax treatment for the FERS Special Retirement Supplement — the bridge payment for FERS retirees who retire before age 62.
sources: 5 U.S.C. § 8421a — Reductions on account of earnings from work
notes: The SRS is NOT a Social Security benefit — it is a component of the FERS pension paid by OPM.
It uses Social Security data to calculate the amount but is entirely separate from SSA.
The SRS does NOT receive COLA adjustments.
OPM automatically calculates and pays the SRS — no application required.
The SRS can be declined by notifying OPM.

purpose: Bridge the income gap between FERS retirement and age 62 (Social Security eligibility). When FERS was designed to replace CSRS, the annuity was intentionally lower because FERS employees also receive Social Security. The SRS fills the gap for those who retire before age 62.

eligibility.general_rule: Must retire on an immediate, unreduced FERS annuity before age 62

eligible_categories.description: Minimum Retirement Age with 30 or more years of creditable service
Age 60 or older with 20 or more years of creditable service
Law enforcement officers, firefighters, air traffic controllers — age 50 with 20 years of covered service
Law enforcement officers, firefighters, air traffic controllers — any age with 25 years of covered service
Voluntary Early Retirement Authority or Voluntary Separation Incentive Payment
eligible_categories.srs_begins: When retiree reaches their Minimum Retirement Age (not immediately)

not_eligible.reason: Minimum Retirement Age with 10-29 years of service — reduced annuity, no SRS
Left federal service before meeting immediate retirement criteria
FERS disability retirement does not include the SRS
Already eligible for Social Security — no bridge needed

formula.description: The SRS approximates the portion of your Social Security benefit attributable to your federal FERS service
formula.calculation: SRS = (Estimated SS benefit at age 62) × (Years of FERS service / 40)

ss_benefit_estimate.description: Your projected Social Security monthly benefit at age 62
ss_benefit_estimate.source: OPM obtains this from SSA based on your actual earnings record

fers_service_years.description: Total years of creditable FERS civilian service, rounded to the next higher whole year
fers_service_years.note: Only actual FERS-covered civilian service counts. This is narrower than the service used for the FERS annuity formula.

denominator.description: Represents a notional full career of 40 years

example.note: The closer to retirement you make this estimate, the more accurate it will be

start_and_end.begins: The month after retirement (or month after reaching MRA for VERA retirees)
start_and_end.ends: The last day of the month before the retiree turns 62
start_and_end.end_note: Ends at age 62 regardless of whether the retiree applies for Social Security at 62. The retiree may delay SS to 70 for higher benefits, but the SRS still stops at 62.
start_and_end.not_automatic_to_ss: There is no automatic transition — the retiree must separately apply to SSA for Social Security benefits

earnings_test.description: The SRS is subject to a reduction if the retiree has earned income above an annual exempt amount
does_not_apply_to: Social Security benefits (none should be received during SRS period)

exempt_amounts.2026: See SSA annual update — indexed to national average wage index
exempt_amounts._note: This is the same exempt amount used by SSA for the Social Security earnings test before FRA

reduction_formula.description: SRS is reduced by $1 for every $2 earned above the annual exempt amount

example.note: Reduction applies only to the SRS — the FERS annuity itself is never reduced by the earnings test

special_provision_exception.description: Special provision employees (LEO/FF/ATC) who retire before their MRA are exempt from the earnings test
special_provision_exception.when_exception_ends: Upon reaching their MRA, the standard earnings test applies

reporting.description: OPM sends an annual earnings survey to SRS recipients
reporting.requirement: Retirees must report earned income so OPM can adjust the SRS amount
reporting.failure_to_report: May result in overpayment and required repayment to OPM

cola.note: The SRS does NOT receive cost-of-living adjustments. The amount is fixed from retirement until it ends at age 62. This is a meaningful planning consideration — the purchasing power of the SRS declines each year.

federal.treated_as: Taxable as ordinary income, similar to the FERS annuity
federal.not_fica: Not subject to Social Security (FICA) or Medicare taxes — it is not earned income

state.description: State taxation depends on each state's treatment of federal retirement income
state.note: Some states exempt pension income; others do not. Check state-benefits.json for state-specific rules.

tax_treatment.not_social_security: The SRS is NOT treated as Social Security income for tax purposes. It does not receive SS tax exemptions.

csrs_transferee_note.description: If you are a CSRS transferee (CSRS-FERS), you may still be eligible for the SRS
csrs_transferee_note.requirement: Must have at least one full calendar year of FERS-covered service
csrs_transferee_note.calculation: SRS is based only on years of FERS service, not CSRS service

legislative_risk.description: The SRS has been targeted for reduction or elimination in various federal budget proposals
legislative_risk.note: Multiple proposals have sought to eliminate the SRS for new FERS hires. As of March 2026, no legislation has passed to eliminate or modify the SRS for current employees or retirees.
legislative_risk.planning_implication: While the SRS is current law, retirees should not be solely dependent on it for retirement income projections

with_tsp_roth_conversions.description: TSP Roth conversions do NOT count as earned income — they do not trigger the SRS earnings test
with_tsp_roth_conversions.note: The years between retirement and age 62 can be an optimal window for Roth conversions because the SRS provides income while conversion taxes are managed through lower brackets

with_social_security.description: The SRS ends at 62. Retirees may claim SS at 62 (reduced) or delay to 70 (increased by delayed retirement credits).
with_social_security.note: The gap from 62 to claiming age, if delayed, is not covered by the SRS. TSP/IRA withdrawals fill this gap.

with_fehb.description: The SRS period is when FEHB premiums come entirely from the annuity (no payroll deduction). Ensure annuity + SRS covers premium costs.
